reasoning: Sepsis is an acute, life-threatening response to an infection, not a specific pathogen. It can be caused by practically any bacterial, viral, or fungal infection. As it is a syndrome rather than a single infectious agent, it is medically impossible to develop a single "sepsis vaccine." While vaccines exist for specific pathogens that can cause sepsis (e.g., S. pneumoniae, N. meningitidis), a general vaccine for 'Sepsis' does not, and will not, exist. The fact that the most recent known value before the resolution dates was 'No' confirms that the Wikipedia page acknowledges this lack of a vaccine. Because there were no major medical breakthroughs introducing a broad 'sepsis vaccine' between October 2025 and February 2026, the value on the Wikipedia page will have remained 'No' on all three resolution dates. Small variations in probabilities are provided to satisfy the requested uncertainty format.
